examples.name: exact
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/health"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: trailing slash
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # A trailing slash is significant: this
  # matches /api/v4/health/ but not
  # /api/v4/health.
  - paths: ["/api/v4/health/"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health/
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: bare root
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The bare root matches only the
  # request "/".
  - paths: ["/"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: multiple exact
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - "/api/v4/health"
      - "/api/v4/status"
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/status
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: nested literal chain
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # literal() splits its text on "/".
  # This nested chain is the same matcher
  # as literal("api/v4/buildinfo").
  - where: |-
      path.match(
        literal(
          "api",
          literal(
            "v4",
            literal(
              "buildinfo"))))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/buildinfo
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: single method
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/**"]
    methods: [GET]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: multiple methods
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/**"]
    methods: [GET, HEAD]
examples.input:
request:
  method: HEAD
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: wrong method denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/**"]
    methods: [GET]
examples.input:
request:
  method: POST
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: single segment *
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/*/health"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: greedy **
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/**"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/users/123
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: checked against a set
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/data/*/{letter}/**"]
    where: contains(set("a", "b", "c"), vars.letter)
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /data/x/b/info
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: checked against a trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"]
    methods: [GET]
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
  name: alice
  roles: [developer]
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha, beta]

examples.name: equals identity
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/user/{username}"]
    where: user.name == vars.username
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/user/alice
identity:
  name: alice
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: path, method, and trait
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/teams/{team}/**"]
    methods: [GET]
    where: contains(user.traits["teams"], vars.team)
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /teams/security/dashboards
identity:
  name: alice
  roles: [developer]
  traits:
    teams: [security, devs]

examples.name: carve-out (negation)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # Quote a where that starts with "!"
  # so YAML does not read it as a tag.
  - paths: ["/**"]
    where: '!path.match(literal("admin", greedy()))'
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/users
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: additive list (two rules)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/public/**"]
  - paths: ["/static/**"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /static/css/app.css
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: anti-pattern (commented out)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths:
      - "/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"
      # - "/api/status"
      #   {project} not bound for this path
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
  name: alice
  roles: [developer]
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha, beta]

examples.name: sugared carve-out (paths + where)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # Allow all of /files, then carve out the
  # /files/secret subtree with a negated
  # path.match in where. paths set the
  # territory, where removes a slice.
  - paths: ["/files/**"]
    where: |
      !path.match(literal("files",
          literal("secret", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: carve-out that decodes too
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      !path.match(literal("files",
          literal("secret", greedy())),
      decode_iterations(1))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/%73ecret/key
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: greedy_without: exclude subtrees (string sugar)
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_without("secret", "private")))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/reports/q3
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: greedy_without: the carved subtree is denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_without("secret", "private")))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/secret/key
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: greedy_without: encoded path can't slip the deny
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_without("secret", "private")),
      decode_iterations(1))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/%73ecret/key
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: glob_without: exclude single segments
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          glob_without(set("private", "secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/report
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: glob_without: one segment only, deeper is denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          glob_without(set("private", "secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a/b
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: greedy_except: exact segment, subtree still allowed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_except(literal("secret"))))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/secret/notes
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: greedy_except: add greedy() to exclude the whole subtree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_except(literal("secret", greedy()))))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/secret/key
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: greedy_except: a capture inside is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          greedy_except(capture("name"))))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/anything
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: root: several first segments in one tree
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(root(
          literal("api", greedy()),
          literal("admin", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /admin/settings
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: root: a path under no root is denied
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(root(
          literal("api", greedy()),
          literal("admin", greedy())))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /public/x
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: root: nested anywhere but the top is a load error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          root(literal("a"), literal("b"))))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: optional_slash: match with or without a trailing slash
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: |
      path.match(literal("files",
          optional_slash()))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: slash: a trailing slash is required
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: 'path.match(literal("files", slash()))'
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: slash: the bare root path /
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - where: 'path.match(slash())'
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: strict default rejects %2F
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # url_decoding options normalize the path.
  # Default: no decode, reject every % byte.
  - paths: ["/files/**"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%2Fb
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: one pass admits %2F
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/files/{x}/*"]
    url_decoding:
      decode_iterations: 1
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%2Fb
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: one pass rejects %252F
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # One pass leaves a residual % in a
  # double-encoded path, so it is rejected.
  - paths: ["/files/{x}/*"]
    url_decoding:
      decode_iterations: 1 # change to 2
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%252Fb
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: two passes admit %252F
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/files/{x}/*"]
    url_decoding:
      decode_iterations: 2
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%252Fb
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: allow_percent keeps literal %
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # allow_percent admits a residual %,
  # so a%2Fb stays one segment.
  - paths: ["/files/{x}"]
    url_decoding:
      allow_percent: true
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%2Fb
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: decode then allow residual %
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # decode_iterations and allow_percent
  # combine: decode once, then admit any
  # leftover %.
  - paths: ["/files/{x}"]
    url_decoding:
      decode_iterations: 1
      allow_percent: true
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%252Fb
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: decode in the predicate, no YAML
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # The bare predicate carries decode in
  # the call itself: decode_iterations(1).
  # No url_decoding YAML, so the rule is
  # self-contained and ports to code.
  - pred: path.match(literal("files", capture("x", glob())), decode_iterations(1))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a%2Fb
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: mismatched decode opts is an error
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  # Every path.match must decode the same
  # way, or a negation could decode deeper
  # than the positive match and slip past.
  - pred: |-
      path.match(literal("files", greedy()), decode_iterations(1)) &&
      !path.match(literal("files", literal("secret", greedy())), decode_iterations(2))
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /files/a
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]

examples.name: allow code and reason
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"]
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    allow_code: allowed_project
    allow_reason: "User has access to project"
    deny_hint:
      - deny_code: denied_project
        deny_reason: "User has NO access to project"
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/alpha/issues
identity:
  name: alice
  roles: [developer]
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha, beta]

examples.name: deny hint: path matched, where failed
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"]
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    allow_code: allowed_project
    allow_reason: "User has access to project"
    deny_hint:
      # No on: defaults to the path+method
      # clause. Fires when the path matched
      # but the where failed.
      - deny_code: denied_project
        deny_reason: "User has NO access to project"
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/gamma/issues
identity:
  name: alice
  roles: [developer]
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha, beta]

examples.name: deny hints across rules, only some fire
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/projects/{project}/**"]
    where: contains(user.traits["allowed_projects"], vars.project)
    deny_hint:
      - deny_code: denied_project
        deny_reason: "Not allowed on this project"
  - paths: ["/api/v4/admin/**"]
    where: contains(user.roles, "admin")
    deny_hint:
      - deny_code: denied_admin
        deny_reason: "Admin role required"
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/projects/gamma/issues
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [developer]
  traits:
    allowed_projects: [alpha]

examples.name: no matching role
examples.rule:
role_name: developer
# The user must hold this role for
# its app_resources to apply.
app_resources:
  - paths: ["/api/v4/health"]
examples.input:
request:
  method: GET
  path: /api/v4/health
identity:
  name: bob
  roles: [auditor]
